_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize 5 TX state legislators (SD-23 West, HD-23 Leo-Wilson, HD-24 Bonnen, SD-24 Flores, HD-25 Vasut). Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. Sources: Texas Legislature Online, Texas Values Action 2025, Texas Alliance for Life 89th, Texas Right to Life, JBS Freedom Index, candidate sites, Ballotpedia, news.

profile.background: Democrat; SD-23 (Dallas) since 1993 (~33 yrs). Managing partner, West & Associates LLP (attorney, Bar since 1979). Ran unopposed 2024; term ends Jan 2029.

royce-west@TX.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from Texas Values Action 2025 floor-vote record (overall 14%), 2021 quorum-break, and roll calls. Pro-choice Democrat with documented contrary votes on life, marriage/sexuality, school choice, and election integrity. CROSS-PARTY CREDIT: voted YES on SB1073 (2025 attorney religious-freedom) — a genuine conscience-exemption vote, scored TRUE. Mixed on Christian-liberty (NO on Ten Commandments SB10/SB1515 and Coach Kennedy SB965, but YES on school prayer SB11). Opposed SB4 (2017 sanctuary-city ban) as 'show me your papers.' Many cells null — no public position found (null does not penalize).

0.note: Pro-choice; voted NO on SB8 (2025 Women's Privacy Act) and SB33 (no taxpayer funds for abortion). Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted against abortion restrictions; does not advocate abolition.

2.note: No public position found on embryonic stem-cell / IVF discard / chimeric research.

3.note: No documented position on euthanasia / assisted suicide.

4.note: No campaign-finance evidence either way on abortion-industry PAC funding.

0.note: Pro-LGBTQ; publicly recanted his SB12 (anti-drag) vote. Does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Documented pro-LGBTQ stance; opposes legal restrictions on LGBTQ relationships.

2.note: Voted NO on HB229 'What is a Woman Act,' SB1257 (detransitioners), SB18 — does not affirm immutable biological sex.

3.note: No documented position on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Recanted SB12 anti-drag vote; voted against LGBTQ-restriction measures in schools/public policy.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA/vouchers); called vouchers 'rooted in segregation.' Opposes school choice.

1.note: Voted NO on SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights).

2.note: Voted NO on SB1396 (sex-ed standards) and SB13 (library-book restrictions).

3.note: No documented position on porn age-verification.

4.note: No documented position on faith-based adoption agencies.

0.note: Voted NO on SB965 (2025 Coach Kennedy / school-employee religious expression).

1.note: CROSS-PARTY: Democrat voted YES on SB1073 (2025 attorney religious-freedom) — a conscience-exemption protection for a profession.

2.note: No documented position on compelled-speech against Christian conviction.

3.note: Voted NO on SB10 (2025) and SB1515 (2023) Ten Commandments displays (public-square Christian symbols). Note: voted YES on SB11 school prayer — mixed, but opposed the symbol-display measures.

4.note: No documented position on state-funded non-Christian displays/curricula.

0.note: No documented position on CBDC / cash / crypto.

2.note: No documented position on balanced-budget amendment / deficit spending.

3.note: No documented position on usury limits / tithe-friendly tax.

4.note: No documented position on WEF/ESG / anti-trust.

0.note: No documented position on hand-counted paper ballots.

1.note: Broke quorum (fled to D.C.) in 2021 to block SB1 election bill, which included voter-ID provisions; opposes restrictive ID/voting measures.

2.note: Opposed SB1 (2021) which restricted early/absentee voting; supports expanded access, not single-day in-person.

3.note: Opposed SB1's limits on mail-in voting and drop boxes; supports expanded mail voting.

4.note: No documented position on private election funding / foreign interference.

0.note: Authored body-camera and racial-profiling laws (accountability, not affirmative pro-police advocacy); no documented defund stance. Held to high bar -> null.

1.note: No documented vote on cashless bail / rogue prosecutors (SB6/Damon Allen Act was authored by Huffman, not West).

2.note: No documented position on victim restitution / proportional punishment.

3.note: No documented position on treating violent crime as a public-health issue.

4.note: No documented statement on the magistrate's duty to bear the sword.

0.note: No documented 10th-Amendment / nullification record.

1.note: No documented record of declining federal mandates.

2.note: No documented resistance to federal overreach on guns/life/education/religion.

3.note: No documented affirmation of state sovereignty against federal action.

4.note: No documented position on federal capture of state elections/data/land.

0.note: No documented vote on border barrier / military presence.

1.note: No documented position on mandatory deportation.

2.note: Voted NO on SB4 (2017 sanctuary-city ban); called it the 'show me your papers law.' Opposes anti-sanctuary preemption.

3.note: No documented position on mandatory E-Verify.

4.note: No documented position on birthright citizenship / foreign ownership.

0.note: Questioned HB1927 (2021 permitless carry) skeptically on the floor, but no confirmed recorded vote located -> held to evidence bar as null.

1.note: No documented vote on red-flag laws / magazine limits / AWB.

2.note: No documented position on NFA/GCA repeal.

3.note: No documented position on castle doctrine / stand-your-ground.

4.note: No documented position on ATF overreach / UN small-arms treaty.

profile.background: Republican; HD-23 (Galveston County) since Jan 2023. Former special-education teacher; three terms (incl. Vice Chair) on the State Board of Education (Dist 6). Texas Alliance for Life 93%; Fiscal Index 93. Re-elected 2024 (~60%); next election Nov 2026.

terri-leo-wilson@TX.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Conservative Republican; took office 2023 so pre-2023 votes (HB1927, SB1-2021) are N/A and scored null. Documented conservative votes via JBS Freedom Index and news: NO on HB1738 (kept homosexual-conduct statute), YES on HB1056 (gold/silver Constitutional Money), YES on HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act), filed foreign-agricultural-land bill, YES on HB2837 (blocks firearm merchant-category-code surveillance), voucher (SB2) champion. Pro-life advocate (TAL 93%, sponsored SB1207 adoption education). No documented liberal-side votes. Several cells null (Christian liberty, public justice, most election cells) — no confirmed individual roll call found.

profile.background: Republican; HD-24 (Friendswood/Galveston County) since 2013. Neurosurgeon; Chair, House Appropriations Committee since 2021. Texas Alliance for Life 97%. Won 2024 GOP primary 83%; next election Nov 2026.

greg-bonnen@TX.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Joint author of HB1280 (2021 abortion 'trigger ban'/Human Life Protection Act); Texas Right to Life Pro-Life Hero; TAL 97%. Supported SB14 (2023 youth gender-medicine ban) final passage. HIGH-VALUE NUANCE on school choice: as Appropriations chair his House stripped/killed ESAs in 2023, but by 2025 he was a House SPONSOR of SB2 (ESA/voucher) and voted YES — scored TRUE on the current (2025) position with the 2023->2025 evolution documented. As Appropriations chair he approved $495.3M for Operation Lone Star (border security). Many cells null — fiscal-hawk groups (YCT) rate Appropriations chairs lower; no balanced-budget-amendment vote, so econ left null (running a constitutionally-required balanced state budget is routine governance, not credited).

profile.religion: Not publicly disclosed (campaign invokes faith generally)
profile.background: Republican; SD-24 since 2023 (previously won SD-19 special 2018, lost it 2020). Retired Texas Parks & Wildlife game warden (27 yrs, rose to Colonel — first Hispanic to hold it). Chair, Senate Criminal Justice Committee. Texas Alliance for Life 100%; YCT 88th = 72. Next election 2026.

pete-flores@TX.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06 from documented votes (SB8/HB100 ESA, SJR5 bail amendment, SB20 AI-CSAM authorship), committee leadership, and cited campaign self-statements (floresfortexas.com/issues). Pro-life (TAL 100%; own statement 'life begins at conception'). Authored SB20 (2025) criminalizing AI-generated child sexual abuse material. Career peace officer chairing Senate Criminal Justice -> affirmative pro-law-enforcement. SJR5 (2025 constitutional bail amendment) passed Senate 31-0 (unanimous incl. Flores). TRANSPARENCY FLAG: YCT rated him 72 (among least-conservative Senate Republicans on fiscal/procedural votes) — but no documented liberal-side vote on any of the 10 issue areas, so no FALSE assigned; marriage/sexuality cells null (no confirmed individual roll calls). Was out of office in 2021, so SB1 election bill and HB1927 carry are N/A.

profile.religion: Attends Second Baptist Church, Angleton; serves as lay preacher
profile.background: Republican; HD-25 (Brazoria County/Angleton) since 2021. Attorney; member, Texas Freedom Caucus; Chair, Redistricting Committee. Texas Right to Life 100%, Texas Values Action 100% 'Faith & Family Champion.' Next election Nov 2026 (primary Mar 3, 2026).

cody-vasut@TX.notes_set: Evidence-scored 2026-06. Hard-right Freedom Caucus member with extensive documented record. Authored HJR15 to abolish abortion (rare clear abolition advocacy); TRTL 100%. Texas Values Action 100%: YES on HB229 'What is a Woman Act,' SB1257 (detransitioners), SB12 (Parents' Bill of Rights), SB13 (library books), SB10 (Ten Commandments), SB11 (school prayer), SB965 (Coach Kennedy); NO on HB1738 (kept homosexual-conduct statute). Coauthored HB1927 constitutional carry. Platform (votevasut.com): state-built border barriers, mandatory E-Verify, opposes sanctuary cities, balanced budget, rejects red-flag laws. No documented liberal-side votes. Public-justice cells null (agent found no bail/police record — not inferred); SB1 individual vote not confirmed -> election cells null.
